_raw_content: You were born soft. Open. Receiving everything. Then the world taught you that feeling was dangerous, so the nervous system did the only thing it knew — it armored. It numbed. It called that survival. But the softness didn't die. It was buried alive beneath the protection. Every unnamed ache, every anxiety without a source — that's not dysfunction. That's your aliveness, still knocking from underneath.
slide_5_revelation: You didn't lose your sensitivity. You buried it alive.

slide_6.text: The nervous system didn't break. It chose survival over feeling. But what it buried is still breathing.

slide_7_cta: Save this if you feel it.
caption:
You were born with your whole system open. Receiving everything.

Then something happened — and feeling became a threat.

So your body did the only thing it knew. It shut down the signal. Not because you were weak. Because you were too alive for what was happening to you.

The numbness was mercy.

But you outgrew the need for it years ago.

That ache you can't name — the one with no story attached — that's not damage. That's your original sensitivity, fully intact beneath the armor, asking permission to come home.

You don't need to heal what was never broken.

You need to unbury what was never dead.
